title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense · Language Clinic: Recovery Ownership
summary: The newest artifact replaces production without a route diff, removed URLs are discovered by readers, rollback requires rebuilding, caches preserve broken assets, ownership ends after deployment, and generated volume is called completion. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the recovery ownership increment in production publication, migration, and…
objectives: Diagnose broken builds, partial deploys, missing routes, bad redirects, stale caches, expired domains, and compromised content with named owners and communication.
Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-recovery-ownership — Diagnose broken builds, partial deploys, missing routes, bad redirects, stale caches, expired domains, and compromised content with named owners and communication.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_release_recovery_defense_3(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Recovery Ownership evidence · Theory Recovery Ownership
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-recovery-ownership-hp830r" that uses case 2010 to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Recovery Ownership evidence · Theory Recovery Ownership.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Recovery Ownership into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Recovery Ownership evidence · Theory Recovery Ownership, keep Recovery Ownership connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-recovery-ownership-hp830r
# Competency: ssg-recovery-ownership.
# Case 2010: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_recovery_ownership_hp830r(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_hp830r = "hp830r-r038ph-2010"
    assert evidence_variant_hp830r
    evidence_axes_hp830r = (
        "readerhp830r",
        "source2010",
        "parser038ph",
        "routehp830r2010",
        "artifact2010hp830r",
        "recoveryr038ph2010",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_hp830r)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Recovery Ownership structural check is only one gate. production publication, migration, and recovery dossier must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Build Recovery Ownership evidence · Theory Recovery Ownership, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Recovery Ownership precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Build Recovery Ownership evidence · Theory Recovery Ownership, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 2357 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Diagnose broken builds, partial deploys, missing routes, bad redirects, stale caches, expired domains, and compromised content with named owners and communication.

For Static-site publishing and Python Build Recovery Ownership evidence · Theory Recovery Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Static sites need no incident or recovery plan.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Recovery Ownership model · Theory Recovery Ownership
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Recovery Ownership: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 6500 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Diagnose broken builds, partial deploys, missing routes, bad redirects, stale caches, expired domains, and compromised content with named owners and communication.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Recovery Ownership model · Theory Recovery Ownership.
steps.why: Retrieving Recovery Ownership through the concrete production publication, migration, and recovery dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Recovery Ownership model · Theory Recovery Ownership, keep Recovery Ownership connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose broken builds, partial deploys, missing routes, bad redirects, stale caches, expired domains, and compromised content with named owners and communication.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-recovery-ownership-hp830r.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-recovery-ownership-hp830r(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_recovery_ownership_hp830r\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?accounted_routes != routes_after)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not recovery_owner)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
